explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The bridge vault contract in handle_jetton_notification (lines 250-274) updates total_burned/total_fees and emits the "BURN" event (which triggers EVM-side minting by off-chain watchers) in the same transaction that merely QUEUES the burn message. On TON, send_raw_message is asynchronous — the actual burn executes in a separate later transaction and can fail/bounce. The bounce handler (lines 448-477) only logs a generic "BNCE" and does NOT revert stats or emit a corrective event. Furthermore, query_id is hardcoded to 0 (line 185), making it impossible to correlate bounces with specific deposits when multiple are in flight. This creates a direct path to unbacked EVM mints: deposit → burn queued + BURN emitted → burn bounces → watcher already minted on EVM based on BURN event → tokens exist on EVM with no corresponding TON burn. Critical severity is warranted as this undermines the core bridge security invariant.
